401.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came, I told him: O Messenger of Allah, Abu Qu'ays came seeking permission to enter but I did not allow him in.' He said: 'He is your paternal uncle — let him come in.' I said: 'It was the woman who nursed me, not the man.' He said: 'He is your uncle — let him come in.'
401.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ datang, aku memberitahunya: Wahai Rasulullah, Abu Qu'ais datang meminta izin masuk tetapi aku tidak mengizinkannya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia adalah pamanmu dari pihak ayah — biarkanlah ia masuk.' Aku berkata: 'Yang menyusuiku adalah wanita, bukan laki-laki.' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia adalah pamanmu — biarkanlah ia masuk.'

402.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The standing of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the two rak'ahs before the Fajr prayer was only as long as it takes to recite Surah al-Fatihah.'
402.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Lamanya berdiri Rasulullah ﷺ dalam dua rakaat sebelum shalat Subuh hanya sebatas waktu yang diperlukan untuk membaca Surah Al-Fatihah.'

403.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that someone asked her about the family of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ eating bread. She said: 'If we had a lamp, we would use its oil as a condiment. There were months when no bread was baked and no pot was cooked for the family of Muhammad ﷺ.'
403.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa seseorang bertanya kepadanya tentang keluarga Rasulullah ﷺ memakan roti. Ia berkata: 'Seandainya kami memiliki lampu, kami akan menggunakan minyaknya sebagai lauk. Ada bulan-bulan di mana tidak ada roti yang dipanggang dan tidak ada panci yang dimasak untuk keluarga Muhammad ﷺ.'

404.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ would recite while sitting, and when he wanted to bow he would stand for the time it takes a person to recite forty verses.'
404.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca sambil duduk, dan ketika ingin ruku beliau berdiri selama waktu yang diperlukan seseorang untuk membaca empat puluh ayat.'

405.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she saw a spear in the house and asked: 'What is this spear for?' She was told: 'We use it to kill geckos.' She then narrated from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that when Ibrahim was thrown into the fire, every creature tried to extinguish it except the gecko — for it was blowing upon it.
405.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia melihat sebuah tombak di rumah dan bertanya: 'Untuk apa tombak ini?' Ia diberitahu: 'Kami menggunakannya untuk membunuh tokek.' Ia kemudian meriwayatkan dari Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa ketika Ibrahim dilemparkan ke dalam api, setiap makhluk mencoba memadamkannya kecuali tokek — karena tokek itu justru meniupkan api tersebut.

406.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked about the verse: 'And they will appear before Allah, the One, the Prevailing' (14:48) — where will the people be on that day? The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The people will be on the Bridge.'
406.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya tentang ayat: 'Dan mereka akan tampil di hadapan Allah Yang Maha Esa lagi Maha Mengalahkan' (14:48) — di mana orang-orang akan berada pada hari itu? Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang-orang akan berada di atas Shiraat.'

407.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked whether the Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted a full month other than Ramadan. She replied: 'By Allah, he never completed a full month of fasting other than Ramadan, nor did he break a full month without fasting some of it.' She was then asked which of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was most beloved to him. She said: 'Abu Bakr.' She was asked who came next. She said: 'Umar.' She was asked who came next. She said: 'Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah.' Then the questioner asked again, and she fell silent.
407.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya apakah Rasulullah ﷺ berpuasa satu bulan penuh selain Ramadan. Ia menjawab: 'Demi Allah, beliau tidak pernah menyelesaikan satu bulan penuh puasa selain Ramadan, dan tidak pernah melewatkan satu bulan penuh tanpa berpuasa sebagiannya.' Ia kemudian ditanya sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ mana yang paling dicintai beliau. Ia berkata: 'Abu Bakar.' Ditanya siapa berikutnya. Ia berkata: 'Umar.' Ditanya siapa berikutnya. Ia berkata: 'Abu Ubaidah bin al-Jarrah.' Kemudian penanya bertanya lagi, dan ia diam.

408.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked about the practice of women going out for the Eid prayer. She replied: 'Young unmarried women used to go out to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ from their seclusion.'
408.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya tentang kebiasaan wanita keluar untuk shalat Eid. Ia menjawab: 'Para perempuan muda yang belum menikah biasa keluar menemui Rasulullah ﷺ dari tempat penyendirian mereka.'

409.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'O Messenger of Allah, is the hatred of meeting Allah the same as hating death? By Allah, we all hate it.' He said: 'No, it is not that. Rather, when a believer is about to be taken by Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, he is given glad tidings of what lies ahead of Allah's reward and honor, so he dies wishing to meet Allah — and Allah wishes to meet him. But when an unbeliever or hypocrite is about to be taken, what awaits him is shown to him...'
409.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah kebencian terhadap pertemuan dengan Allah sama dengan membenci kematian? Demi Allah, kami semua membencinya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak, bukan itu yang dimaksud. Melainkan, ketika seorang mukmin akan dijemput oleh Allah Yang Maha Mulia lagi Maha Agung, ia diberi kabar gembira tentang apa yang menanti berupa pahala dan kemuliaan dari Allah, sehingga ia wafat dalam keadaan ingin bertemu Allah — dan Allah pun ingin bertemu dengannya. Namun ketika orang kafir atau munafik akan dijemput, apa yang menunggunya diperlihatkan kepadanya...'

410.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I used to twist the garlands for the sacrificial animals of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he would not leave any of his needs from his wife until the pilgrims returned.'
410.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku biasa menganyam kalung untuk hewan kurban Rasulullah ﷺ, dan beliau tidak meninggalkan kebutuhannya dari istrinya hingga para jamaah haji kembali.'

411.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The prayer of a woman who has reached puberty is not accepted without a head covering.'
411.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat seorang wanita yang telah mencapai usia haid tidak diterima tanpa penutup kepala.'

412.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Allah does not accept the prayer of a woman who has reached puberty except with a head covering.'
412.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah tidak menerima shalat seorang wanita yang telah mencapai usia haid kecuali dengan penutup kepala.'

413.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'No one has asked me about the verse "Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it" (4:123) since I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about it.' He said: 'O Aisha, this refers to Allah pursuing His servant with every fever, stumble, and thorn that strikes him — even a merchant who misplaces his goods in his sleeve and panics when he cannot find them, then discovers them folded against him — until the believer comes out of his sins as pure gold emerges from a furnace.'
413.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Tidak ada seorang pun yang bertanya kepadaku tentang ayat "Barang siapa mengerjakan kejahatan maka akan dibalas karenanya" (4:123) sejak aku bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ tentangnya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Wahai Aisyah, ini mengacu pada Allah yang mengejar hamba-Nya dengan setiap demam, tersandung, dan duri yang menimpanya — bahkan seorang pedagang yang kehilangan barangnya di dalam lengan bajunya dan panik ketika tidak menemukannya, lalu mendapatinya terlipat di sisinya — hingga orang beriman keluar dari dosa-dosanya seperti emas murni keluar dari dapur peleburan.'

414.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to bathe with a sa' of water and perform ablution with a mudd.
414.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mandi dengan satu sha' air dan berwudhu dengan satu mudd.

415.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that it was mentioned to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that some people disliked facing the qiblah when relieving themselves. He said: 'Have they really done that? Turn my seat to face the qiblah.'
415.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa disebutkan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa beberapa orang tidak menyukai menghadap kiblat saat buang air besar. Beliau bersabda: 'Benarkah mereka melakukan itu? Putar tempat dudukku menghadap kiblat.'

416.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'When we entered Makkah, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever wishes to make it an Umrah may do so, except those who have brought a sacrificial animal." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ slaughtered cows on behalf of his wives on the Day of Sacrifice. When it was the night of al-Batha, she became pure. She asked: O Messenger of Allah ﷺ, will my companions return with both Hajj and Umrah while I return with only Hajj? So he commanded Abd al-Rahman...'
416.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika kami memasuki Makkah, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barang siapa yang ingin menjadikannya sebagai Umrah, maka lakukanlah, kecuali mereka yang membawa hewan kurban." Rasulullah ﷺ menyembelih sapi atas nama para istrinya pada Hari Idul Adha. Ketika malam al-Batha tiba, ia telah suci. Ia bertanya: Wahai Rasulullah ﷺ, apakah teman-temanku akan kembali dengan haji dan umrah sementara aku hanya kembali dengan haji? Maka beliau memerintahkan Abd al-Rahman...'

417.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Among the most severely punished people on the Day of Resurrection are those who imitate the creation of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.'
417.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Di antara orang-orang yang paling berat azabnya pada Hari Kiamat adalah orang-orang yang meniru ciptaan Allah Yang Maha Mulia lagi Maha Agung.'

418.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that a garment of black wool was brought to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he put it on. When he sweated and noticed the smell of wool, he discarded it. She added: he liked pleasant fragrance.
418.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa sebuah pakaian dari wol hitam dibawa kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau memakainya. Ketika beliau berkeringat dan mencium bau wol, beliau melepasnya. Ia menambahkan: beliau menyukai wewangian yang harum.

419.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked about al-irak, and she asked: 'What is al-irak?' Then she struck the shoulder of her companion and said: 'Stop — you have hurt your brother.' Then she said: 'What is al-irak? It is menstruation. Say what Allah says: the menstrual period.' She then said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ would put his arm around me and put his head against mine while between him and me was a garment — and I was menstruating.'
419.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya tentang al-irak, dan ia bertanya: 'Apa itu al-irak?' Kemudian ia memukul bahu temannya dan berkata: 'Berhenti — kamu telah menyakiti saudaramu.' Lalu ia berkata: 'Apa itu al-irak? Itu adalah haid. Ucapkanlah apa yang Allah katakan: periode haid.' Ia kemudian berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa merangkulku dan menyandarkan kepalanya pada kepalaku sementara di antara beliau dan aku ada sehelai kain — dan aku sedang haid.'

420.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ, whenever he passed by my door, would say a word by which Allah benefits people. One day he passed and said nothing. Then he passed again and said nothing — twice or three times. I said to my maid: Set a cushion for me at the door, and I tied a cloth around my head. He passed and said: O Aisha, what is the matter with you? I said: I have a headache. He said: I too have a headache.'
420.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ, setiap kali melewati pintuku, biasa mengucapkan sebuah kata yang dengannya Allah memberi manfaat kepada orang-orang. Suatu hari beliau melewatinya dan tidak berkata apa-apa. Kemudian beliau melewatinya lagi dan tidak berkata apa-apa — dua atau tiga kali. Aku berkata kepada pelayanku: Letakkan bantal untukku di pintu, dan aku membalut kepalaku dengan kain. Beliau melewati dan berkata: Wahai Aisyah, ada apa denganmu? Aku berkata: Kepalaku sakit. Beliau berkata: Aku pun kepalaku sakit.'

421.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed while wearing a woolen shawl — part of it over him and part over her.
421.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ shalat dengan mengenakan selimut wol — sebagian darinya menutupi beliau dan sebagian lagi menutupinya.

422.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ slaughtered his sacrificial animals on Eid, he would buy two large, fat, horned, black-and-white, castrated rams. He would slaughter one of them on behalf of his ummah — those who affirmed the oneness of Allah and bore witness that he had conveyed the message — and slaughter the other on behalf of Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.'
422.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ menyembelih hewan kurbannya pada Idul Adha, beliau membeli dua ekor domba jantan yang besar, gemuk, bertanduk, berbulu hitam-putih, dan dikebiri. Beliau menyembelih satu di antaranya atas nama umatnya — mereka yang menegaskan keesaan Allah dan bersaksi bahwa beliau telah menyampaikan risalah — dan menyembelih yang lain atas nama Muhammad dan keluarga Muhammad.'

423.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I never saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ rush to anything as he rushed to the two rak'ahs before Fajr — or to booty.'
423.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah melihat Rasulullah ﷺ bersegera melakukan sesuatu sebagaimana beliau bersegera melakukan dua rakaat sebelum Subuh — atau menuju ghanimah.'

424.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'The best of what a man eats is from his own earnings, and his child is among his earnings.'
424.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha), dari Nabi ﷺ, bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik apa yang dimakan seorang laki-laki adalah dari hasil usahanya sendiri, dan anaknya termasuk dari usahanya.'

425.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to kiss while fasting.'
425.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mencium sementara beliau sedang berpuasa.'

426.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) through a chain of narrators including al-Suddi or Ziyad ibn Alaqa — this being a hadith that his son Abd al-Rahman mentioned to him.
426.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) melalui rantai perawi termasuk al-Suddi atau Ziyad bin Alaqah — ini adalah hadis yang disebutkan oleh anaknya Abd al-Rahman kepadanya.

427.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'The prayer of a sitting person is worth half the prayer of one who stands.'
427.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha), dari Nabi ﷺ, bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Shalat orang yang duduk nilainya setengah dari shalat orang yang berdiri.'

428.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The prayer of a sitting person is worth half the prayer of one who stands.'
428.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat orang yang duduk nilainya setengah dari shalat orang yang berdiri.'

429.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), with a raised chain, that she said: 'The prayer of a sitting person — not sitting cross-legged — is worth half the prayer of one who stands.'
429.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha), dengan sanad marfu', bahwa ia berkata: 'Shalat orang yang duduk — bukan dalam posisi bersila — nilainya setengah dari shalat orang yang berdiri.'

430.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she and Hafsa agreed that whichever of them the Prophet ﷺ visited would say: 'I detect the smell of maghafir on you — have you eaten maghafir?' He visited one of them and she said that. He replied: 'No, I drank honey at Zaynab bint Jahsh's and I will not do so again.' Then the verse was revealed: 'O Prophet, why do you prohibit what Allah has made lawful for you?' (66:1) — referring to Aisha and Hafsa.
430.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia dan Hafsah sepakat bahwa siapa pun di antara mereka yang dikunjungi Nabi ﷺ akan berkata: 'Aku mencium bau maghafir darimu — apakah engkau makan maghafir?' Beliau mengunjungi salah satu dari mereka dan ia berkata demikian. Beliau menjawab: 'Tidak, aku minum madu di tempat Zainab binti Jahsy dan aku tidak akan melakukannya lagi.' Kemudian turunlah ayat: 'Wahai Nabi, mengapa engkau mengharamkan apa yang Allah halalkan bagimu?' (66:1) — merujuk kepada Aisyah dan Hafsah.

431.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Prophet ﷺ would go out to the Fajr prayer with his head dripping from a bath due to ritual impurity, and then complete his fast that day.'
431.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ biasa keluar untuk shalat Subuh dengan kepalanya masih menetes air dari mandi janabah, kemudian menyempurnakan puasanya hari itu.'

432.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was intimate with one of his wives, then slept while in a state of ritual impurity until morning, then he bathed and fasted that day.'
432.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ berhubungan intim dengan salah satu istrinya, kemudian tidur dalam keadaan junub hingga pagi, lalu beliau mandi dan berpuasa hari itu.'

433.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked by the Prophet ﷺ about something suspicious, and she was hesitant. He said: 'Tell me, or the All-Knowing, the All-Aware will inform me.' She said: 'O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be your ransom' — and she told him. He said: 'So you were the dark shape I saw in front of me?' She said: 'Yes.' He pushed her on the back and said: 'Did you think that Allah and His Messenger would wrong you?' She said: 'Whatever people hide, Allah knows.' He said: 'Yes, for Jibril came to me when I saw...'
433.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ menanyainya tentang sesuatu yang mencurigakan, dan ia ragu-ragu. Beliau berkata: 'Beritahulah aku, atau Yang Maha Mengetahui lagi Maha Mengenal akan memberitahuku.' Ia berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, semoga ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusanmu' — dan ia memberitahunya. Beliau berkata: 'Jadi kamu adalah sosok gelap yang aku lihat di depanku?' Ia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau mendorongnya di punggungnya dan berkata: 'Apakah kamu mengira Allah dan Rasul-Nya akan berbuat zalim kepadamu?' Ia berkata: 'Apa pun yang disembunyikan orang, Allah mengetahuinya.' Beliau berkata: 'Ya, karena Jibril datang kepadaku ketika aku melihat...'

434.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that when some Companions expressed longing for their homelands, she asked each of them about Madinah. She then came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and informed him of what they said. He looked up at the sky and said: 'O Allah, make Madinah beloved to us as you made Makkah beloved to us — or more so. Bless us in our sa' and mudd.'
434.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika beberapa sahabat mengungkapkan kerinduan terhadap tanah air mereka, ia bertanya kepada masing-masing tentang Madinah. Ia kemudian menemui Rasulullah ﷺ dan memberitahukan apa yang mereka katakan. Beliau memandang ke langit dan bersabda: 'Ya Allah, jadikanlah Madinah dicintai kami sebagaimana Engkau jadikan Makkah dicintai kami — atau lebih dari itu. Berkahilah kami dalam sha' dan mudd kami.'

435.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Prophet ﷺ used to pray thirteen rak'ahs every night: nine standing, and two while seated. Then he would wait until the first call for Fajr, and then pray two rak'ahs.'
435.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ biasa shalat tiga belas rakaat setiap malam: sembilan rakaat sambil berdiri, dan dua rakaat sambil duduk. Kemudian beliau menunggu hingga azan pertama Subuh, lalu shalat dua rakaat.'

436.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray thirteen rak'ahs at night, including the two rak'ahs of Fajr.
436.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa shalat tiga belas rakaat di malam hari, termasuk dua rakaat Subuh.

437.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that a woman with prolonged bleeding was brought to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. She said: 'I saw her basin full of blood.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to her: 'Stay for as long as your period normally holds you back, then bathe and pray.'
437.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa seorang wanita dengan pendarahan berkepanjangan dibawa kepada Rasulullah ﷺ. Ia berkata: 'Aku melihat basinnya penuh dengan darah.' Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Tinggallah selama periode haidmu biasanya menahan dirimu, kemudian mandilah dan shalatlah.'

438.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked whether the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when ritually impure, would simply wash his head or pour water all over it. She replied: 'He would pour water all over his head.'
438.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya apakah Rasulullah ﷺ ketika junub hanya mencuci kepalanya atau menuangkan air ke seluruh kepalanya. Ia menjawab: 'Beliau menuangkan air ke seluruh kepalanya.'

439.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to her regarding Usamah: 'Remove the harm from him.' She said she found it repulsive, but the Messenger of Allah ﷺ then sucked it out and spat it out. He then said: 'If Usamah were a girl, I would dress him and adorn him until he was married off.'
439.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya mengenai Usamah: 'Hilangkan gangguan darinya.' Ia berkata ia merasa jijik, namun kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ mengisapnya dan memuntahkannya. Beliau kemudian bersabda: 'Seandainya Usamah adalah perempuan, aku akan mendandani dan menghiasi dia hingga ia dinikahkan.'

440.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was asked whether the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite poetry. She replied: 'Sometimes he would recite the poetry of Ibn Rawahah, and he would say: "The one who did not provision you will bring you news."'
440.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya apakah Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca puisi. Ia menjawab: 'Terkadang beliau mengutip puisi Ibnu Rawahah, dan beliau berkata: "Orang yang tidak membekalimu akan membawa berita untukmu."'

441.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that a camel of charity was sent to her and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Aisha, hold fast to fear of Allah and gentleness, for gentleness is never present in something except that it beautifies it, and never removed from something except that it makes it ugly.'
441.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa seekor unta sedekah dikirim kepadanya dan Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai Aisyah, berpeganglah pada ketakwaan kepada Allah dan kelembutan, karena kelembutan tidak pernah ada dalam sesuatu melainkan ia memperindahnya, dan tidak pernah dicabut dari sesuatu melainkan ia membuatnya buruk.'

442.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he saw wind or clouds coming, would turn to face them and say: 'O Allah, a beneficial rain.' But when the wind stirred strongly and the clouds gathered, he would seek refuge in Allah from its evil.
442.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika melihat angin atau awan datang, berpaling menghadapnya dari mana pun ia datang dan mengucapkan: 'Ya Allah, hujan yang bermanfaat.' Tetapi ketika angin bertiup kencang dan awan berkumpul, beliau berlindung kepada Allah dari keburukannya.

443.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she described the beginning of revelation. Khadijah said to Waraqa: 'O uncle, listen to the son of my brother.' Waraqa said: 'O son of my brother, what do you see?' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ told him what he had seen. Waraqa said: 'This is the Namus — the one who was sent down to Musa. Would that I were young! Would that I were alive when your people drive you out!' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked: 'Will they drive me out?'
443.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia menggambarkan awal turunnya wahyu. Khadijah berkata kepada Waraqah: 'Wahai paman, dengarkanlah putra saudaraku.' Waraqah berkata: 'Wahai putra saudaraku, apa yang engkau lihat?' Rasulullah ﷺ menceritakan kepadanya apa yang beliau lihat. Waraqah berkata: 'Ini adalah Namus — yang diturunkan kepada Musa. Andai aku masih muda! Andai aku masih hidup ketika kaummu mengusirmu!' Rasulullah ﷺ bertanya: 'Apakah mereka akan mengusirku?'

444.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that one night Sawda bint Zam'ah, wife of the Prophet ﷺ, went out for a need, and Umar saw her — for she was a tall woman. He called out: 'Indeed we have recognized you, O Sawda!' — eager for the hijab verse to be revealed. Aisha said: 'So the verse of hijab was revealed.'
444.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa suatu malam Saudah binti Zam'ah, istri Nabi ﷺ, keluar untuk suatu keperluan, dan Umar melihatnya — karena ia adalah wanita yang tinggi. Ia berseru: 'Sesungguhnya kami telah mengenalmu, wahai Saudah!' — ia sangat ingin ayat hijab diturunkan. Aisyah berkata: 'Maka ayat hijab pun turun.'

445.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) through a chain of narrators from Ibn Abi Dhi'b from al-Zuhri — with the same chain and meaning.
445.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) melalui rantai perawi dari Ibnu Abi Dzi'b dari al-Zuhri — dengan sanad dan makna yang sama.

446.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Those who make these images will be punished on the Day of Resurrection and will be told: "Give life to what you created."'
446.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang-orang yang membuat gambar-gambar ini akan diazab pada Hari Kiamat dan akan dikatakan kepada mereka: "Hidupkanlah apa yang kalian ciptakan."'

447.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), wife of the Prophet ﷺ, that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not pray the Duha prayer. I pray it. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ abandoned many acts of worship out of fear that people would follow him in them and they would become obligatory upon them.'
447.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha), istri Nabi ﷺ, bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ tidak shalat Dhuha. Aku shalat Dhuha. Rasulullah ﷺ meninggalkan banyak amalan ibadah karena khawatir orang-orang akan mengikutinya dan amalan-amalan itu akan menjadi wajib bagi mereka.'

448.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), wife of the Prophet ﷺ, that she said: 'By Allah, whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was given a choice between two matters, he always chose the easier of the two — as long as it was not sinful. When it was sinful, he was the farthest of all people from it. By Allah, he never took personal revenge for anything done to him — unless the sanctities of Allah were violated, in which case he would take revenge for the sake of Allah.'
448.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha), istri Nabi ﷺ, bahwa ia berkata: 'Demi Allah, setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ diberi pilihan antara dua perkara, beliau selalu memilih yang lebih mudah di antara keduanya — selama itu bukan dosa. Ketika itu dosa, beliau adalah orang yang paling jauh darinya di antara semua orang. Demi Allah, beliau tidak pernah membalas dendam pribadi atas apa pun yang dilakukan kepadanya — kecuali jika kehormatan-kehormatan Allah dilanggar, maka beliau mengambil tindakan demi Allah.'

449.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'Sometimes I would twist the garlands for the sacrificial animals of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He would garland his animals and send them off, then remain without abstaining from anything that a person in ihram abstains from.'
449.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Terkadang aku menganyam kalung untuk hewan kurban Rasulullah ﷺ. Beliau mengalungkan hewan-hewannya dan mengirimnya, kemudian tetap tinggal tanpa berpantang dari apa pun yang dipantang oleh orang yang berihram.'

450.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'It is as if I can still see the gleam of perfume in the parting of the hair of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was proclaiming the talbiyah.'
450.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Seakan-akan aku masih bisa melihat kilau wewangian di belahan rambut Rasulullah ﷺ sementara beliau mengucapkan talbiyah.'
